channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran and the US hold a second round of negotiations over Tehran's nuclear program in Rome
item.description: ROME (AP) — Iran and the United States held a second round of negotiations Saturday over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program in Rome, U.S. and Iranian officials said. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia vacation town braces for mayhem as crime-filled beach fest returns
item.description: Orange Crush is expected to draw tens of thousands of people to Tybee Island, Georgia, this Easter weekend. Event organizers promise to keep the event safe and clean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Amid the abandoned chemistry notes and other debris left behind after a deadly shooting at Florida State University are lingering questions about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 250 years after America went to war for independence, a divided nation battles over its legacy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The American Revolution began 250 years ago, in a blast of gunshot and a trail of colonial spin. Starting with Saturday's anniversary of the Battles of Lexington and Concord, the country will look back to its war of independence and ask where its legacy stands today.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Four centuries later, Easter's date remains divisive. Some church leaders want that to change
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — For more than 400 years, Catholic and Orthodox churches have used different ways to determine the date of Easter. But this Sunday will mark a special moment for Christians, as the churches celebrate of Jesus’ resurrection on the same day.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, COLLEEN BARRY and LEFTERIS PITARAKIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US senator returns from El Salvador trip, says Abrego Garcia case is about far more than one man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The dispute over the wrongful deportation and imprisonment of Kilmar Abrego Garcia “is not only about one man” but about Donald Trump's disregard of the American judicial system as well, Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: "Mario Kart World" and "Donkey Kong Bananza" will launch alongside the console, with prices holding steady at $79.99 and $69.99, respectively. Digital and physical editions will be offered at the same price point.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster and other allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: ACLU asks Supreme Court to block ‘imminent’ wave of Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) asked the Supreme Court on Friday to immediately intervene to block the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants being held in Texas to El Salvador under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Snickers Pizookie coming to BJ’s Restaurants
item.description: The Broccoli Cheddar Pizookie, slated to debut at BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse nationwide, won’t be coming to restaurants after all. Instead, BJ’s will be releasing a Snickers Pizookie. “We've come to our senses.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slain Texas teen Austin Metcalf’s home swatted hours after tense press conference showdown
item.description: A swatting took place at Jeff Metcalf's home just hours after he was criticized by a spokesman for the family of his son's alleged murderer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge to allow 'bushy eyebrows' testimony in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A judge says a former roommate of four University of Idaho students who were killed in 2022 can testify about seeing an intruder with “bushy eyebrows” around the time of the crime.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho judge denies Bryan Kohberger's 'bushy eyebrows' motion
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's trial will proceed with key evidence after a judge denied a defense request to block eyewitness testimony about "bushy eyebrows" in the Idaho student murders case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US has 800 measles cases and outbreaks in several states. Here's what you should know
item.description: The U.S. has 800 cases of measles nationwide as of Friday, and two more states identified outbreaks this week. Texas is driving the high numbers, with an outbreak centered in West Texas that started nearly three months ago and is up to 597 cases.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sen. Cornyn asks Trump for more funds for flood-stricken South Texas
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn, of Texas, sent a letter Friday to President Trump asking for more help for flood-stricken South Texas, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s claims on Kilmar Abrego Garcia largely rely on 1 informant
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's immigration troubles started in 2019, when he was arrested outside of a Maryland Home Depot.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's plans to open 21 new locations across 13 states
item.description: Is your neighborhood lacking a grocery store that could easily double a well-lit tiki bar? That may change in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: Two people were killed and at least six others were wounded when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, and police said a 20-year-old suspect — the son of a sheriff's deputy — was shot and taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: FDA hiring contractors to replace terminated safety inspection staffers
item.description: When Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced sweeping job cuts at his department last month, he said that safety inspectors who oversee U.S. foods and drugs wouldn't be impacted.
item.dc:creator: By MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: "I am further appalled and furious that I now have to explain pornography to my fourth grader," the mom said.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4
